title: [lichen_l2.c:994 + lora_l2.c:506] rx_mutex not reinitialized after thread abort leads to deadlock
description:
Cross-file bug: After a forced RX thread abort, lora_l2's deinit() reinitializes lora_mutex (line 542) but lichen_l2.c's rx_mutex is NOT reinitialized. If the abort occurred while the thread held rx_mutex (inside lichen_l2_input at line 994), subsequent operations will deadlock.

Sequence:
1. RX thread receives packet, enters lichen_l2_input(), acquires rx_mutex (line 994)
2. Thread hangs (e.g., lichen_link_rx blocks)
3. stop() times out, calls k_thread_abort()
4. Thread killed while holding rx_mutex
5. User calls lichen_lora_l2_deinit() - clears aborted flag, reinits lora_mutex
6. User calls lichen_lora_l2_init() - succeeds
7. User calls lichen_l2_enable(true)
8. needs_reinit() returns false (cleared by deinit)
9. enable() registers callback, starts new RX thread
10. New thread receives packet, calls callback -> lichen_l2_input()
11. lichen_l2_input() tries k_mutex_lock(&rx_mutex) - DEADLOCK

Root cause: lora_l2.c and lichen_l2.c are tightly coupled but handle recovery independently. lora_l2 can recover its own state (lora_mutex), but lichen_l2's rx_mutex requires separate handling.

Fix options:
A) lichen_l2 needs its own deinit() function that reinitializes rx_mutex and tx_mutex, called before lichen_lora_l2_deinit()
B) lora_l2.c's needs_reinit() flag should NOT be cleared by deinit() - require a full module reinitialization that includes lichen_l2
C) Document that thread abort requires full system reboot (already hinted at line 535)
close_reason: Fixed: Added lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() function to reinitialize rx_mutex during deinit recovery. Called from lora_l2_deinit() after lora_mutex reinitialization.
